========================================================================
  CLAUDE SEMAFORO v1.6.0 -- Install guide (Windows / Linux / macOS)
========================================================================

A visual traffic light for Claude Code's status inside VSCode:
  RED    = Claude is working
  AMBER  = Claude needs you (permission or question)
  GREEN  = ready, you can type a new prompt
  GREY   = no active session in this project

You also get a native DESKTOP NOTIFICATION when the status changes AND
the VSCode window is NOT focused (so you notice while working elsewhere).
It fires only for "waiting" (Claude needs you) and "idle" (Claude ready).
You can tweak this in Settings (search "Claude Status"):
  - notifications.enabled          (on/off, default on)
  - notifications.sound            (play a sound, default on)
  - notifications.onlyWhenUnfocused (default on)
  - notifications.states           (default: waiting, idle)

The amber (waiting) light BLINKS to grab your attention; red breathes
gently; green and grey stay steady.

Archive contents:
  - claude-semaforo-1.6.0.vsix       -> the VSCode extension
  - claude-state-hook.js             -> the hook that writes the status
  - claude-settings-example.json     -> example hook configuration
  - INSTALL.txt                      -> this guide

The .vsix alone only shows the UI. You ALSO need the hook and the
settings, otherwise the light stays grey.

Requirement: Node.js must be installed and on your PATH (the hook runs
with `node`).

------------------------------------------------------------------------
 1) INSTALL THE EXTENSION
------------------------------------------------------------------------
From a terminal, in the folder where you extracted this archive:

    code --install-extension claude-semaforo-1.6.0.vsix

(or in VSCode: Extensions -> "..." menu -> Install from VSIX...)

------------------------------------------------------------------------
 2) COPY THE HOOK INTO ~/.claude/hooks/
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Windows (PowerShell):

    New-Item -ItemType Directory -Force "$HOME\.claude\hooks"
    Copy-Item claude-state-hook.js "$HOME\.claude\hooks\"

Linux / macOS:

    mkdir -p ~/.claude/hooks
    cp claude-state-hook.js ~/.claude/hooks/

------------------------------------------------------------------------
 3) CONFIGURE THE HOOKS IN ~/.claude/settings.json
------------------------------------------------------------------------
If you DON'T have a settings.json yet, just copy the example:

    Windows:  Copy-Item claude-settings-example.json "$HOME\.claude\settings.json"
    Linux:    cp claude-settings-example.json ~/.claude/settings.json

If you ALREADY have a settings.json, open claude-settings-example.json
and MERGE its "hooks" section into your own settings.json (keep your
existing settings).

The 7 configured events:
    SessionStart, UserPromptSubmit, PreToolUse, PermissionRequest,
    PostToolUse, Stop, Notification

NOTE: the hook command is written as
    node -e "require(require('os').homedir()+'/.claude/hooks/claude-state-hook.js')" <Event>
This is intentional so it works IDENTICALLY on Windows and Linux
(Node resolves the home directory, not the shell).

------------------------------------------------------------------------
 4) RESTART
------------------------------------------------------------------------
  - Restart Claude Code   (to load the hooks)
  - Reload VSCode         (Developer: Reload Window)

You'll find the traffic light:
  - as an ICON in the sidebar (activity bar) -> graphical view
  - in the STATUS BAR at the bottom-left -> compact status

------------------------------------------------------------------------
 PER-PROJECT STATE
------------------------------------------------------------------------
Each window/project has its own independent light (the state is written
to <tmp>/claude-semaforo/<hash-of-folder>.json). Two Claude sessions in
the SAME folder share the same state.
========================================================================
